calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-12-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Continued To Jan-24-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Sever
Off Calendar Dec-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Sever.

Standard Insurance Company's motion to sever, to which Plaintiff Donglin Li filed a notice of non-opposition, is granted. The record does not show grounds for mandatory joinder of, on the one hand, the writ of mandate claim against the California Department of Insurance and the Commissioner of the California Department of Insurance and, on the other hand, the contract and bad faith claims against Standard Insurance Company. Nor does the record show a basis for permissive joinder.

The fourth cause of action for writ of mandamus against the California Department of Insurance and the Commissioner of the California Department of Insurance is ordered severed from the other claims in Plaintiff's complaint, filed July 13, 2023. The Clerk is directed to assign a new case number to the severed cause of action (the fourth cause of action). The other causes of action against Standard Insurance Company will proceed under the existing case number.

Moving party's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. (Evid. Code, Secs. 451 and 452.) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "Although the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, the truth of statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable." (Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont Gen. Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
